featured.title: Where to Watch the 2026 World Cup in Berlin
featured.label: Featured
featured.summary: The 2026 World Cup is in North America and Berlin has no Brandenburg Gate fan mile this year. Where to actually watch it: free screens, beer gardens and bars, with every kick-off in Berlin time.
featured.alt: A summer evening football public viewing in Berlin with a relaxed crowd, outdoor screen and TV Tower skyline

posts.title: Visiting Berlin in June: A Walking Guide's Local Take
posts.summary: What June in Berlin is actually like, from a local guide: long days, beer gardens, swimming lakes, free festivals, and the history behind the month.
posts.alt: Berlin skyline with the TV Tower at sunset and people sitting by the river

posts.title: Where Was the Berlin Wall? An Interactive Map of East and West in 1989
posts.summary: The Berlin Wall ran 155 kilometers around West Berlin. Tap any point on this interactive map to see exactly where it stood and which side an address was on in 1989.
posts.alt: Remaining stretch of the Berlin Wall standing in the city today

posts.title: Berlin Public Transport 2026: U-Bahn, S-Bahn, Tram & Bus
posts.summary: Berlin's U-Bahn, S-Bahn, tram and bus all share one ticket system. The zones, tickets and prices a tourist needs in 2026, plus where to buy and how to reach BER.
posts.alt: Yellow Berlin train speeding through a U-Bahn station platform
